title: Acontias pentaphyllus
taxonomy: Acontias pentaphyllus is a species of flat-skinned, burrowing lizard in the family Cordylidae. This species is endemic to Southern Africa, specifically in the provinces of Northern Cape and Western Cape in South Africa. Acontias pentaphyllus is classified under the kingdom Animalia, phylum Chordata, class Reptilia, order Squamata, and family Cordylidae. The species is often referred to as the " flat-skinned lizard" due to its unique flat, scaleless body. Acontias pentaphyllus is a relatively small species of lizard, typically reaching a length of around 8-12 centimeters.

intro: Acontias pentaphyllus, also known as the 'Mountain Aconite', is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Asia, belonging to the Aconitum genus. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering potential health benefits such as pain relief, anti-inflammatory properties, and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Acontias pentaphyllus include alkaloids like aconitine, aconitine, and neoline, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
